segments.text: [Music]
I've got a Slack window open on the
right and I've got a Sonic Cube cloud
project on the left.
Let's start by inviting SonaCube into
the Slack channel. Then let's look at
the help. This queries the application
to understand all the different commands
that are available at this point. There
are really three steps. connect, invite,
and subscribe.
Let's check that we're connected.
Now, let's subscribe to a project. You
need to grab the project key. An easy
way to do this is to grab it from the
URL in the Sonic Cloud project.
Great. It's now set up. So, whenever the
quality gate status changes, anyone
subscribed to that Slack channel will
now get a notification.
So let's simulate a change to the Sonarq
project with an analysis that fails the
quality gate. You will see the green
tick in the Sonarq project change to a
fail status and a corresponding message
arrives in Slack.
And if we simulate the quality gate
changing again, you will see another
message in Slack.
And just like that, your Sona Cube
alerts are now flowing directly into
your Slack channel, making sure your
team receives immediate feedback.
There's a direct link to the official
SonarCube docs in the description below.
If this guide was helpful, please give
it a thumbs up and consider subscribing
for more Sona Cube tutorials.
Thanks for watching and happy coding.

fullText: [Music] I've got a Slack window open on the right and I've got a Sonic Cube cloud project on the left. Let's start by inviting SonaCube into the Slack channel. Then let's look at the help. This queries the application to understand all the different commands that are available at this point. There are really three steps. connect, invite, and subscribe. Let's check that we're connected. Now, let's subscribe to a project. You need to grab the project key. An easy way to do this is to grab it from the URL in the Sonic Cloud project. Great. It's now set up. So, whenever the quality gate status changes, anyone subscribed to that Slack channel will now get a notification. So let's simulate a change to the Sonarq project with an analysis that fails the quality gate. You will see the green tick in the Sonarq project change to a fail status and a corresponding message arrives in Slack. And if we simulate the quality gate changing again, you will see another message in Slack. And just like that, your Sona Cube alerts are now flowing directly into your Slack channel, making sure your team receives immediate feedback. There's a direct link to the official SonarCube docs in the description below. If this guide was helpful, please give it a thumbs up and consider subscribing for more Sona Cube tutorials. Thanks for watching and happy coding.
